authoritative_sources.title: General Compliance Program Guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OIG is the federal healthcare oversight authority for fraud, waste, abuse, exclusions, and compliance program guidance; the GCPG is OIG's public reference for compliance infrastructure, risk assessment, reporting, investigations, auditing, monitoring, and remediation.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Compliance program infrastructure, obligation mapping, auditing, monitoring, reporting, investigation intake, and remediation
used_for: legal, fraud, and regulator-facing escalation boundaries

authoritative_sources.title: Summary of the HIPAA Privacy Rule
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR implements and enforces the HIPAA Privacy Rule, making this source authoritative for PHI, covered-entity and business-associate handling, individual rights, permitted uses and disclosures, and privacy officer boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: PHI-sensitive hospitality, travel, guest, patient, and member evidence handling
used_for: privacy-sensitive itinerary and lodging request handling

authoritative_sources.title: Minimum Necessary Requirement
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: HHS OCR is the HIPAA enforcement authority and this official guidance constrains routine and non-routine PHI requests, disclosures, evidence excerpts, and reasonable-reliance handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Minimum-necessary evidence packets and approved retrieval handling
used_for: refusal when unnecessary PHI is pasted into chat or shared with lodging partners

authoritative_sources.title: Medical Review and Education
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers Medicare medical review and identifies claim documentation, NCD/LCD/manual source hierarchy, medical review contractor operations, coverage, coding, billing, and medical-necessity boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Claim, documentation, coverage, authorization, medical review, and payer-interpretation evidence handling
used_for: payer interpretation and medical necessity escalation

authoritative_sources.title: Organization Determinations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS is the Medicare payer authority and this guidance defines Medicare Advantage authorization, payment, cost-sharing, quantity-limit, and appeal-related decision boundaries.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Eligibility, benefit, authorization, payment, appeal, and payer-owner routing
used_for: lodging benefit and authorization prerequisite scenarios

authoritative_sources.title: CMS' Discharge Planning Rule Supports Interoperability and Patient Preferences
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public payer or provider operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CMS administers Medicare and Medicaid Conditions of Participation; this official source identifies discharge planning, post-acute care selection, necessary medical information transfer, and care-transition expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Discharge, care-transition, post-acute selection, and travel support evidence handling
used_for: clinical-versus-administrative boundary for travel after discharge
handoff to licensed clinical and patient-safety owners

authoritative_sources.title: Common Formats
authoritative_sources.publisher: Agency for Healthcare Research and Quality
authoritative_sources.source_class: Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: AHRQ is the federal agency for healthcare quality and patient safety, and Common Formats are recognized public event-reporting formats and terminology for adverse events, near misses, and unsafe conditions.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Patient/member safety signal classification and event escalation
used_for: adverse event, near miss, and unsafe condition terminology
refusal to close safety signals as guest-service issues

authoritative_sources.title: Americans with Disabilities Act Title III Regulations
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality and travel operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: DOJ is the primary federal civil-rights authority for ADA Title III enforcement; the lodging reservation provisions directly govern accessible-room descriptions, reservation blocking, and accessibility-sensitive hospitality controls.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Accessible lodging reservation controls and accessibility-sensitive evidence handling
used_for: accessible room and reservation evidence requirements

authoritative_sources.title: The Rule on Unfair or Deceptive Fees: Frequently Asked Questions
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality and travel operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: FTC is the primary federal consumer-protection regulator and this official short-term lodging guidance defines total-price, mandatory-fee, truthful-fee-description, and final-payment disclosure expectations.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Short-term lodging fee disclosure and guest billing control review
used_for: lodging fee and charge evidence expectations

authoritative_sources.title: Travel-associated Outbreaks as a Public Health Issue
authoritative_sources.publisher: Centers for Disease Control and Prevention
authoritative_sources.source_class: Public hospitality and travel operating guidance
authoritative_sources.authority_rationale: CDC is the primary U.S. public-health authority and this accommodation-specific guidance addresses travel-accommodation exposure investigation, guest-list requests, and notification handling.
authoritative_sources.workflow_stage: Travel-accommodation public-health escalation and evidence handling
used_for: accommodation outbreak and exposure stop conditions

workflow_stages.name: Request intake and regulated context grounding
workflow_stages.description: Capture request intent, tenant policies, jurisdiction, facility, provider type, service setting, payer, plan, benefit, patient/member status, service dates, authorization, referral, consent, minimum-necessary PHI handling, booking channel, lodging partner, systems of record, and owner matrix before producing compliance outputs.

Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public hospitality and travel operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Obligation, policy, and control mapping
workflow_stages.description: Map OIG, OCR, CMS, AHRQ, DOJ, FTC, CDC, payer, state, tenant, and lodging partner obligations to controls, evidence artifacts, evidence owners, review cadence, and escalation triggers.
source_classes: Public healthcare regulator guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public hospitality and travel operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Healthcare-adjacent hospitality evidence assembly
workflow_stages.description: Validate eligibility, benefits, authorization, referral, encounter, order, discharge, care-transition, reservation, itinerary, lodging partner, charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, access-log, privacy, and remediation artifacts against named systems of record.
source_classes: Public healthcare regulator guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Public hospitality and travel operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Finding classification and boundary triage
workflow_stages.description: Classify findings as documentation gaps, control failures, missing authorization/referral/eligibility dependencies, privacy-sensitive matters, payer interpretation blockers, lodging partner control gaps, possible fee/accessibility issues, clinical-risk signals, or patient/member safety signals requiring escalation.
source_classes: Public healthcare regulator guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public hospitality and travel operating guidance

workflow_stages.name: Exception, remediation, and review preparation
workflow_stages.description: Prepare evidence packets, obligation matrices, compliance finding summaries, control-gap trackers, remediation notes, review or attestation preparation packets, and assumption logs without claiming legal, clinical, payer, privacy, accessibility, public-health, or certification signoff.
source_classes: Public healthcare regulator guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance

workflow_stages.name: Escalation, refusal, and adjacent-owner handoff
workflow_stages.description: Refuse or escalate legal advice, regulator response, accessibility legal conclusions, public-health clearance, privacy breach signoff, payer interpretation, coding selection, claim approval, diagnosis, treatment, medical necessity, discharge safety, clinical travel clearance, and patient/member safety resolution while preserving evidence for authorized owners.
source_classes: Public healthcare regulator guidance
Public payer or provider operating guidance
Recognized healthcare workflow or measure guidance
Public hospitality and travel operating guidance

artifacts: Tenant policies, policy hierarchy, approval matrix, partner lodging standards, compliance control catalog, audit plan, annual work plan, and regulator or payer source references
Eligibility, benefits, authorization, referral, coverage, payer portal, organization determination, medical review, service date, and plan records
EHR or EMR encounter records, orders, clinical-administrative notes, care-transition records, discharge planning records, consent references, and minimum-necessary handling notes

Reservation records, itinerary records, lodging partner attestations, PMS or booking-engine records, accessible-room feature records, guest communications, room-blocking evidence, folio or charge records, and lodging fee disclosures
Charge capture, coding, claim, remittance, denial, appeal, billing audit, revenue cycle, and reimbursement support documentation

Access logs, break-glass logs, CRM profile access records, travel profile records, privacy case records, role-access approvals, PHI redaction logs, retention or legal-hold flags, and data-sharing agreements

Incident reports, patient/member complaints, missed-care or delayed-service reports, adverse event or near-miss records, unsafe-condition reports, CDC-related accommodation exposure notices, public-health guest-list requests, and safety escalation records
Remediation plans, exception logs, corrective-action records, retest evidence, evidence readiness checklists, review packets, owner approvals, and closure evidence
systems_of_record: EHR or EMR and associated clinical documentation systems
Practice management, revenue cycle, claim, denial, remittance, and billing audit systems
Payer portal, eligibility, benefit, authorization, referral, and organization-determination systems
Case management, utilization review, care-transition, discharge planning, CRM, or patient/member access systems
PMS, booking engine, reservation platform, lodging partner portal, itinerary, and guest communications systems
Compliance case management or GRC system
Policy management and document repository with retention, legal-hold, and audit trail controls
Identity, access-log, break-glass, CRM access, and SIEM systems used for healthcare access evidence
Quality reporting, QAPI, patient/member safety, and incident management systems

allowed: Classify hospitality-and-travel compliance requests as in scope, out of scope, or blocked on missing prerequisites.
Request and validate tenant facts, source context, authoritative records, approved retrieval hooks, systems of record, and approval owners.
Map obligations to controls, evidence artifacts, evidence owners, review cadence, and escalation triggers.
Determine whether visible evidence is complete, missing, stale, conflicting, untraceable, or blocked by required owner review.
Prepare control or obligation matrices, evidence checklists, gap summaries, review-preparation packets, exception escalation notes, and remediation trackers.
escalate: Tenant policy, jurisdiction, facility, provider type, service setting, payer, plan, benefit, lodging partner, booking channel, patient/member status, service date, authorization, referral, coverage, consent, or minimum-necessary handling facts are missing.

EHR, EMR, practice management, payer portal, authorization, referral, access-log, PMS, lodging partner, booking, privacy, billing, claim, audit, or compliance records conflict or cannot be tied to a system of record.
Public guidance, payer rules, tenant policy, state rules, lodging partner terms, operational practice, or record systems conflict without an approved precedence path.

Legal, regulator-facing, payer interpretation, coding, billing, utilization management, revenue integrity, privacy officer, licensed clinical, patient/member safety, lodging operations, accessibility legal, public-health, or executive authority is required.
refuse: Request seeks legal advice, privileged investigation conclusions, self-disclosure decisions, direct regulator negotiation, accessibility legal conclusion, public-health clearance, or formal certification.
Request asks the lane to sign off that billing, privacy, fraud, overpayment, payer, lodging, accessibility, or regulator-sensitive issue is legally resolved.
Request requires diagnosis, treatment planning, medical necessity, level-of-care, clinical travel clearance, discharge safety, clinical appropriateness, or patient/member safety resolution.
Request exposes unnecessary PHI in an unapproved channel or asks for PHI handling outside approved role, purpose, consent, data-sharing agreement, or minimum-necessary limits.
Request asks for coding selection, claim approval, payer-policy interpretation, lodging benefit approval, refund or fee waiver, or partner policy override.

domain_failure_modes: Treating hospitality compliance as generic hotel GRC and ignoring healthcare-specific records such as EHR, claims, authorizations, referrals, eligibility, access logs, PHI controls, medical review requests, care-transition records, and patient/member safety reports.

Treating healthcare compliance as generic provider compliance and ignoring hospitality/travel artifacts such as reservations, lodging partner attestations, accessible-room feature evidence, itineraries, guest communications, room-blocking records, folios, and lodging fee disclosures.
Closing a required control when evidence is incomplete, stale, not tied to a system of record, or missing owner approval.
Letting guest-service urgency or patient satisfaction bypass authorization, referral, eligibility, consent, PHI, payer, documentation, lodging partner, or remediation controls.
Resolving conflicts among CMS, OIG, OCR, DOJ, FTC, CDC, payer policy, state law, tenant policy, lodging partner terms, clinical records, and operational practice without authorized precedence.

Exposing PHI or privacy-sensitive itinerary, diagnosis, MRN, appointment, room, caregiver, or access-log details outside approved retrieval, role-access, consent, data-sharing, or minimum-necessary constraints.
Providing legal, privacy breach, payer, coding, billing, utilization, regulator-facing, accessibility, public-health, or clinical conclusions outside lane authority.

Misclassifying missed care, delayed transport, discharge travel risk, or unsafe lodging conditions as simple guest-service or documentation issues and failing to route to licensed clinical or patient/member safety owners.
Applying one public baseline across states, payer products, facility types, service settings, booking channels, and lodging partners without tenant-specific facts.

scenario_family_coverage.scenario_family: Missing authorization, referral, or eligibility dependency
PHI or privacy-sensitive request that requires controlled handling
Boundary case that would require licensed clinical or payer-interpretation escalation
Missing evidence for a required control or obligation
Request for legal interpretation, regulator negotiation, or formal signoff
A required control is missing evidence
Request for formal legal interpretation or signoff outside lane authority
Normal in-scope execution using realistic industry terminology and artifact shapes
Boundary rejection when the request should move to an adjacent specialist
Low-confidence handling when required facts, records, or authority sources are missing
Conflicting-source or conflicting-policy handling that requires escalation instead of confident synthesis
Tenant-specific adaptation handling that preserves the public baseline and records assumptions

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: The queue slug is hospitality-and-travel while the resolved industry profile and domain research pack are healthcare-and-care-delivery.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack defines the lane as hospitality-and-travel compliance where guest, traveler, patient, or member service workflows intersect with healthcare and care-delivery obligations. It uses hospitality/travel sources plus the required healthcare source classes.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Tenant-specific policy, state law, payer contract, facility type, service setting, lodging partner term, and booking-channel precedence vary materially.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The pack requires runtime retrieval and escalation instead of guessing. It blocks case-specific conclusions when these facts are missing or conflicting.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Payer-specific authorization, referral, benefit, coverage, coding, medical review, and reimbursement requirements change by plan and date.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: Public CMS sources provide the baseline, but current payer plan guidance and tenant payer contracts must be retrieved before case-specific conclusions.

unresolved_ambiguities.topic: Licensed clinical, patient/member safety, privacy breach, accessibility legal, public-health, and regulator-facing authority may overlap in serious travel or lodging incidents.
unresolved_ambiguities.notes: The lane refuses final clearance or signoff and prepares evidence-preserving escalation packets for designated owners. No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains for validated specification-based deployment.

required_workflow_questions.question: What prerequisites or authorizations gate the workflow?
required_workflow_questions.answer: Tenant policy hierarchy, jurisdiction, business unit, facility, provider type, service setting, payer or plan, lodging benefit, patient/member eligibility, service dates, stay dates, referral status, authorization status, coverage rule reference, lodging partner approval, booking channel, consent, minimum-necessary PHI handling, and approved retrieval hooks gate case-specific output.

required_workflow_questions.question: Which records or systems establish the patient, member, or claim truth set?
required_workflow_questions.answer: EHR or EMR encounter records, practice management records, billing and claim systems, payer portal, authorization and referral systems, eligibility and benefits systems, case management, utilization review, patient/member access or CRM systems, PMS or booking records, privacy case management, access logs, compliance case management, audit-tracking, and document repositories establish the truth set.

required_workflow_questions.question: Where does the lane stop and licensed clinical, privacy, or payer-interpretation authority begin?
required_workflow_questions.answer: The lane stops at evidence handling, gap classification, obligation mapping, review preparation, remediation tracking, and escalation. Licensed clinical owners decide diagnosis, treatment, medical necessity, discharge safety, clinical travel clearance, and patient/member safety resolution; privacy owners decide breach or access-violation conclusions; payer/revenue integrity/coding/billing/utilization owners decide coverage, authorization, coding, medical review, reimbursement, and claim-payment interpretation.
